Home / FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions About Winsome Products and Services

Do you manufacture blinds locally in Georgia? +

Yes. Winsome is positioned as a local manufacturer in Tbilisi, which allows for a more precise fit, flexible timelines, and better control over the final quality. Production reflects the specifics of the Georgian market, and our local approach lets us respond quickly to individual requests. We serve Tbilisi, Batumi, Kutaisi, Rustavi, Gori, and other cities across Georgia.

Do you help choose fabric, slat, or mesh systems? +

Yes. During consultation we review room type, light intensity, privacy needs, profile color, and the system that will be most practical for each opening. For example, a bedroom may benefit from blackout fabric, while a kitchen may need a moisture-resistant, easy-to-clean option. Consultation is free—you can reach us by phone or WhatsApp.

Do you offer measuring and installation? +

Yes. The full process includes consultation, on-site measuring, custom production, and professional installation. Measuring is done on site so every detail—including opening shape, profile type, and installation space—is accounted for. After installation we check system operation and provide care recommendations.

Are pleated blinds suitable for small windows? +

Yes. Pleated systems are especially practical for small, narrow, and non-standard windows, including skylights or triangular openings. The compact folding mechanism does not take up extra space and keeps small rooms from feeling heavy. Winsome produces pleated blinds to custom measurements, so even unusual windows can be covered cleanly and functionally.

Can pleated blinds be made to custom measurements? +

Yes. Winsome manufactures every order based on accurate on-site measuring—each window is measured individually rather than using stock sizes. Measuring service is available in Tbilisi and major cities across Georgia. Production begins after measurement and is typically completed within several working days. You receive blinds cut to your exact opening for smooth operation and a neat finish.

Are roller blinds a good choice for bedrooms? +

Yes. Roller blinds are one of the most popular options for bedrooms. Depending on your needs you can choose light-filtering (dimout) or full blackout fabric. Blackout is especially useful when the window faces a street or when you need complete darkness for children’s rooms or shift workers. During consultation we help you pick the right opacity and colour for sleep quality and daytime comfort.

Do you offer a wide range of colours and fabrics? +

Yes. Winsome’s roller blind catalogue includes more than 70 colours and fabric options—neutral, pastel, and bolder tones. Various textures are available, including smooth, linen-effect, and other finishes. At consultation you can review samples and choose a combination that harmonises with your furniture and wall colours. This makes it easier to match new blinds to an existing renovation or a full redesign.

Are zebra blinds practical for offices? +

Yes. Zebra (day-night) blinds are very practical in office settings. The dual-layer fabric lets you regulate daylight without fully raising the blind, which helps reduce glare on screens and monitors. The contemporary look fits open-plan offices as well as private meeting rooms. Operation is quick for staff who open and close blinds often during the day. Colour and fabric choice can align with corporate interiors or residential-style studio spaces.

What is the difference between zebra blinds and day-night blinds? +

They are the same product category: alternating sheer and opaque stripes on two layers that slide past each other. “Zebra” refers to the striped look; “day-night” describes how you can switch between softer daylight and stronger privacy in one headrail. At Winsome we size the system to your reveal depth and help you pick stripe scale and fabric opacity for living rooms, kitchens, or bedrooms. Installation is planned so the chain or wand clears handles and sill projections. That way you get smooth operation and a clean stack whether the blind is fully lowered or partially open.

Are Roman blinds a good choice for bedrooms? +

Yes. Roman blinds are an excellent bedroom option when you want a soft, textile-rich, cosy look. Unlike curtains alone, they fold up compactly and do not consume unnecessary wall or stack space. Both translucent and blackout fabrics are available depending on how much morning light you want. The structured fold also gives a tailored, finished appearance that works in both classic and modern bedrooms.

Can Roman blinds be made with blackout fabric? +

Yes. Roman blinds are available with both light-filtering and full blackout fabrics. Blackout is especially useful for bedrooms and children’s rooms where complete darkness improves sleep. Fabric type is chosen during consultation based on orientation, street lighting, and your preferences. Winsome helps you balance aesthetics with the level of shading you need year-round.

Are faux wood blinds an alternative to real wood blinds? +

Yes. Faux wood blinds deliver a warm look close to natural timber while staying practical and cost-effective. Compared with solid wood, they are typically lighter, need less specialised maintenance, and cope better with humidity in kitchens or bathrooms when specified appropriately. They suit homeowners who love wood aesthetics but prioritise durability and easy cleaning. Winsome can advise on room suitability during measuring.

Ready to refresh your space?

Get in touch and we will help you choose the right blinds, roller shades, or insect screen system for your windows and doors.

Request a consultation

Winsome creates calm, practical, precisely fitted solutions for homes, offices, and commercial spaces.
